title: SQL Injection on /search Endpoint
description: The /search endpoint is vulnerable to SQL injection. User-supplied input is directly concatenated into SQL queries without sanitization, allowing an attacker to extract data, bypass authentication, and potentially gain internal network access through database features like xp_cmdshell or COPY TO PROGRAM.

title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) — Reflected
description: Multiple parameters reflect user input without encoding. An attacker can inject malicious JavaScript that executes in victim's browser sessions.

title: Directory Listing Enabled
description: Apache directory listing is enabled, exposing internal file structure, configuration files, and potentially sensitive data to unauthenticated users.

title: Server Version Disclosure
description: The web server exposes its version in HTTP response headers (Apache/2.4.7 on Ubuntu). This helps attackers identify known vulnerabilities for the specific version.

title: Missing Security Headers
description: Several security headers are missing: X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Content-Security-Policy. This makes the application susceptible to clickjacking and MIME-type attacks.
